sutta: 186. now a certain monk came to visit the exalted 1 and on coming to him saluted the exalted 1 and sat down at 1 side as he said thus that monk said this to the exalted 1 pray lord by what is the world led by what is the world drawn along under the sway of what that has arisen does it go and the buddha said well said well said sadhu sadhu happy is your approach to a question goodly is you're ready with and lovely is your questioning your question is thus is it not pray lord by what is the world led by what is the world drawn along under the sway of what that has arisen does it go and the monk said it is lord well monk the world is led by mind chitta by mind it is drawn along by mind when mind has arisen it goes under its way that is well said lord replied that monk welcoming what was said by the exalted 1 and after thanking him asked another question ask as to the common saying widely learned 1 who knows dhamma by heart pray lord of what sword is 1 widely learned who knows dhamma by heart well said well said monk happy is your approach to a question goodly is he already with and lovely is your questioning you ask this do you not of what sort is 1 widely learned who knows dharma by heart i do lord well monk i have i have taught dharma widely now if a monk understands the meaning and text of dhamma even if if it be but a stanza of 4 lines and be set on living in accordance with dhamma he may well be called 1 widely learned who knows dhamma by heart it is well said lord replied that monk welcoming what was said by the exalted 1 and after thanking him asked another question as to the common saying lord learned of penetrative wisdom pray a lord of what sort is 1 learned of penetrative wisdom well said well said monk happy is your approach to a question etc you ask this do you not of what sort is 1 learned of penetrative wisdom i do lord in this case among hears it said this is [Music] by wisdom he penetrates the meaning of that saying and sees that it is so he hears it said this is the arising of dukkha this is the ending of dukkha this is the practice going to the ending of dukkha by wisdom he penetrates the meaning of that saying and sees that it is so thus monk he is learned of penetrative wisdom it is well said lord replied that monk and put yet another question as to the common saying wise of great wisdom pray lord of what sword is 1 who is wise of great wisdom well said well said monk [Music] etc as before you ask this do you not who is wise or great wisdom i do lord well monk in this case he who is wise of great wisdom thinks not with a view to harm either himself or another or both alike so thinking he thinks with a view to the prophet of self of another both of self and of another to the prophet of the whole world tasman 1 is wise of great wisdom that's the end of the suta
commentary: so you see in this suta you can see the buddha's method the buddha's loving kindness when this monk asks him a question said do sadhu to the monk and praise the manga i said happy is your approach to a question goodly is you're ready with and lovely is your questioning then he replied the monk so the first 1 he said that the world is led along by thought or by mind cheetah this word cheetah can can mean thought it can mean mind so because our mind is active that the the the world is is uh is is basically in our mind is drawn along by mind so by this is men that the buddha said that the ma the world actually arises in our sixth consciousness when our mind the sixth consciousness works then you you get the world and when the 6 consciousness stops there is no world so the buddha said that the world arises dependent on uh the sixth consciousness on the the mind then the second 1 um the buddha said now a monk is called widely learned who knows dhamma by heart if he knows even a few lines of dhamma but he lives according to dhamma so it's very important not only to know dharma but to live in accordance to dhamma then you are widely learned and you know dharma by heart and the third 1 1 who is learned of penetrative wisdom is 1 who understands the 4 aryan truths then because he understands the 4 aryan truths his wisdom is penetrative then the fourth 1 is wise of great wisdom is 1 who does not harm himself does not harm others he's a harmless person and you can have that now if you don't have that ego you don't have that great self because the great self brings a long selfishness and when we have selfishness uh to benefit ourselves sometimes we harm others so that's the end of this
